title: St Paul's Steeple
content:
Upon Paul's steeple stands a tree
    As full of apples as may be,
    The little boys of London town
    They run with hooks to pull them down;
    And then they run from hedge to hedge
    Until they come to London Bridge.
meaning:
Walter Crane's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "St Paul's Steeple"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
